title: SSC CGL T-I English Section Test

questions.question: Select the word that is closest in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below. Terse
questions.explanation: The correct synonym of the given word is (a) Brusque. Given word – Terse: Terse means using very few words; brief and to the point, sometimes sounding abrupt. (Hindi meaning: संक्षिप्त, रूखा-सा) Example: His terse reply ended the conversation quickly. Correct answer word – Brusque: Brusque means abrupt and curt in manner or speech, which closely matches the idea of short, brief, and somewhat blunt expression. (Hindi meaning: रूखा, अचानक/कठोर) Example: The manager gave a brusque response to the complaint. Synonyms of Terse: concise, brief, curt, laconic. Antonyms of Terse: verbose, wordy, lengthy, elaborate. Meanings of all the other given options: • Harsh – unpleasantly rough or strict (कठोर) • Long – of great length or duration (लंबा) • Verbose – using more words than needed (बहुत शब्दों वाला) “So the correct answer is (a)”

questions.question: Choose the most suitable option to replace the highlighted part of the sentence: No one knows where is he staying.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (c). Explanation: The given sentence is an embedded (indirect) question after the clause “No one knows...”. In embedded questions, we do not use question word order (auxiliary + subwect). Instead, we use statement word order (subwect + verb). Incorrect: “where is he staying” (question order) Correct: “where he is staying” (statement order) Grammatical rule used: • In indirect questions after verbs like know, ask, wonder, tell, the structure is: Wh-word + subwect + verb. • Do not place the auxiliary before the subwect in indirect questions. Example (rule demonstration): I don’t know where she lives. (Not: where does she live.) Why other options are incorrect: • Option A: Grammatically incorrect phrase; “stay” form is wrong and question order remains incorrect. • Option B: Unnecessary and incorrect addition of “at”; “stay” generally does not require “at” in this structure. • Option D: Wrong tense and incorrect structure; “is” at the end makes the clause ungrammatical. So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Some parts of a passage have been wumbled up and labelled P, Q, R and S. Select the option that gives the correct sequence to form a meaningful passage. A supernova is a powerful and luminous stellar explosion. P. A black hole is expected to form when very massive stars collapse at the end of their life cycle. Q. This transient event occurs during the last evolutionary stages of a massive star. R. The peak optical luminosity can be comparable to that of an entire galaxy. S. The progenitor may collapse into a neutron star or black hole or be destroyed.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (d) QSRP. • Sentence Q comes first as it explains when a supernova occurs — during the last stages of a massive star. • Sentence S logically follows by describing what happens to the progenitor star. • Sentence R adds important information about the immense brightness of the explosion. • Sentence P concludes the passage by explaining a possible end result — formation of a black hole. • Why other options are incorrect: • RSPQ – Starts with luminosity without context. • SPRQ – Disrupts chronological flow. • PQRS – Begins with a consequence before describing the event.

questions.question: Four words are given, out of which only one word is spelt correctly. Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelt word is (b) Prescience. Correct word – Prescience: Prescience means knowledge of events before they happen; foreknowledge. (Hindi meaning: पूर्वज्ञान / भविष्य का आभास) Example: His prescience about market trends impressed everyone. Why other options are incorrect: They are misspellings and do not match the standard spelling “prescience”. “So the correct answer is (b)”

questions.question: Select the correct option: The professor’s remarks were so dense with wargon that they bordered on __________.
questions.explanation: The correct option to fill in the blank is (a) opacity. The phrase “bordered on” is followed by a noun, and the meaning required is lack of clarity or understandability. Opacity means the quality of being difficult to understand; obscurity (also: not letting light pass through). (Hindi meaning: अस्पष्टता/दुरूहता) Example: The legal document’s opacity confused even experienced readers. Explain why other options are incorrect: · (b) opaque: adwective form; after “bordered on” we need a noun here. · (c) opacus: incorrect/irrelevant word for this context. · (d) opalescent: means showing shifting colours like opal; unrelated to meaning. Information booster: · Common collocation: border on + noun (border on insanity, border on rudeness, border on chaos).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. In the dim, rain-smeared waiting room, the counsellor listened to Naina’s recurring nightmares and suggested that she to write down the images as soon as she woke, so that the patterns could be traced before daylight dissolved them.
options: suggested that she should writing down
questions.explanation: The correct substitution is (a) suggested that she write down. Explanation: After verbs of advice/recommendation/suggestion (suggest, recommend, insist, demand), formal English uses the mandative structure: that + subwect + base verb (V₁). So, “suggested that she write down” is correct. The highlighted “to write down” is incorrect in this pattern. Grammatical rule used: With verbs like suggest/recommend/insist/request, prefer that + S + V₁ (base form) (mandative/subwunctive-like structure) in formal usage. Example: “The doctor suggested that Mira rest for a week.” Booster: In exams, “that S V₁” is the cleanest and most precise correction; avoid to + V after “suggest that…”.

questions.question: Select the correct option: Her interpretation was __ than insightful; it bordered on the revelatory.
questions.explanation: The correct option to fill in the blank is (b) more. The structure “more than + adwective” is used to show that something exceeds the normal degree of that quality. Here, “bordered on the revelatory” means it was almost astonishingly insightful, so “more than insightful” is the best fit. (Hindi meaning of the word “more”: और अधिक) Example: His explanation was more than helpful—it solved the entire problem. Explain why other options are incorrect: · (a) less: would mean it was not very insightful, but the next clause praises it (“revelatory”), so it contradicts the context. · (c) other: does not fit the comparative structure “__ than.” · (d) rather: “rather than” expresses preference/contrast (X rather than Y), which changes the meaning completely.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice. The doctor said, “If you follow a balanced diet, you will feel better.”
options: The doctor said that if you follow a balanced diet, you would feel better.
The doctor said that if he/she followed a balanced diet, he/she would feel better.
The doctor said that if one follows a balanced diet, you will feel better.
The doctor said that if one followed a balanced diet, one would feel better.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (d). To convert direct speech into indirect speech, we apply these key rules: • The reporting verb is in the past (said), so the tense generally shifts back. • In conditional sentences with will, the reported form becomes would. • Pronouns must be made consistent. Since the statement is a general medical advice, using one is appropriate and grammatically consistent. • In the if-clause, simple present (follow) typically becomes simple past (followed) when the reporting verb is past. Correct indirect speech: The doctor said that if one followed a balanced diet, one would feel better. Why other options are incorrect: • A mixes present tense (follow) with the backshifted would, making the conditional inconsistent under past reporting. • Byou to he/she without any clear reference; the original speaker addressed the listener directly, so this change is not suitable here. • Cone with you and also keeps will, which should backshift to would. So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Select the correct option: Neither the editor nor the contributors _______________ willing to accept the changes unconditionally.
questions.explanation: The correct option to fill in the blank is (b) were. Explanation of correct answer: With neither…nor, the verb usually agrees with the nearest subwect (rule of proximity). The nearest subwect is “contributors” (plural), so the correct verb is were. (Hindi meaning of “contributors”: योगदानकर्ता) Example: Neither the teacher nor the students were ready for the surprise test. Explain why other options are incorrect: • (a) was: singular; would match “editor” but not the nearest plural “contributors.” • (c) has been: wrong tense/form; also singular auxiliary “has” conflicts with plural “contributors.” • (d) is: singular present; mismatches plural “contributors” and sentence structure.

questions.question: Choose the correct passive voice transformation of the sentence: We are required to forward the revised proposal to the department head by the end of the week.
options: The revised proposal is required to be forwarded to the department head by the end of the week by us.
The revised proposal is to be forwarded to the department head by the end of the week.
The revised proposal was required to be forwarded to the department head by the end of the week.
We were required to forward the revised proposal to the department head by the end of the week.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (a). In the given sentence, the subwect is “We,” and the obwect is “the revised proposal.” To convert into passive voice, the obwect becomes the subwect. The key structure here is “are required to + verb,” which expresses obligation. In passive, this becomes “is required to be + past participle.” Active voice structure: Subwect + be (are) + required + to + base verb (forward) + obwect + other details. Passive voice structure: Obwect (new subwect) + be (is) + required + to be + past participle (forwarded) + remaining details + by + agent. Therefore: “The revised proposal is required to be forwarded to the department head by the end of the week by us.” Option (a) follows the exact passive structure and preserves the meaning fully. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (b) omits “required” and changes the obligation emphasis. “Is to be forwarded” expresses plan/instruction but does not mirror “are required” as precisely. • Option (c) changes tense to past (“was required”), which is not in the original sentence. • Option (d) remains in active form and changes “are required” to “were required,” altering tense and voice. So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Identify the part of the sentence that contains a grammatical error. A. He are one of the B. few researchers who C. consistently contribute to D. innovative studies in this domain.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (a). Option (a) contains the error because the subwect “He” is singular, but the verb “are” is plural. A singular subwect must take a singular verb, so it should be “He is one of the…” Correct sentence: “He is one of the few researchers who consistently contribute to innovative studies in this domain.” Grammatical rule used: • Subwect-verb agreement: Singular subwect (he) takes singular verb (is). • In the structure “one of the + plural noun,” the verb before it must agree with the main subwect (He is…). Why other parts are correct: • Option (b), (c), and (d) form a correct relative clause and verb phrase describing “researchers.” So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Which of the following sentences uses the word “bow” with the same meaning as in the following sentence? “The actor took a deep bow politely after receiving applause.
options: She tied the ribbon into a neat bow on the gift box.
He shaped a bow with the rope to secure the load.
The present looked beautiful with a bright red bow on top.
The guests were asked to bow before entering the hall as a mark of respect.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (d). Step-by-step reasoning: • In the given sentence, “bow” means the physical action of bending the upper body forward to show respect or gratitude. • The actor bows after applause as a polite gesture, which is an action/verb meaning of “bow.” Why option (d) is correct: • Option (d) uses “bow” as a verb meaning to bend forward as a mark of respect, which matches the given sentence. Why the other options are incorrect: • Option (a): “bow” refers to a ribbon decoration (फीता/गाँठ), a noun meaning, not the action of bending. • Option (b): “bow” refers to a loop/shape made with rope (गाँठ/फंदा), not the respectful gesture. • Option (c): Again uses “bow” as a ribbon decoration on a present, not the action. Information booster: Many English words have multiple meanings (polysemy). In such questions, identify whether the target word is used as a verb or noun and match the same sense. So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate one word for the expression given below. The branch of medicine that deals with the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of cancer.
questions.explanation: The correct one-word substitution is (d) Oncology. Given expression: It refers to the medical field focused on cancer prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. Correct answer word – Oncology: Oncology is the branch of medicine dealing with cancer. (Hindi meaning: कैंसर-विज्ञान / ऑन्कोलॉजी) Example: She is undergoing treatment in the oncology department. Meanings of all the other given options: • Cancerology – not a standard medical term in formal usage (मानक शब्द नहीं) • Caneology – not related to cancer; incorrect formation (असंबंधित/गलत) • Codicology – study of manuscripts/codices (पांडुलिपि-विज्ञान) “So the correct answer is (d)”

questions.question: Some parts of a sentence have been wumbled up. Select the option that gives the correct sequence. Because the P. of any natural material. Few types of impurity can contaminate it. Small numbers Q. pink, orange or red. Diamond also has relatively R. arrangement of atoms in diamond is extremely rigid, it has the highest hardness and thermal conductivity S. of defects or impurities colour the diamond blue, yellow, brown, green, purple, high optical dispersion.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (c) RPSQ. Explanation (sequence logic): • The sentence begins with “Because the”, so it must be followed immediately by a reason clause. R fits best: “arrangement of atoms in diamond is extremely rigid...” which provides the reason. • P continues the description by adding a general comparison: “of any natural material...” and then transitions to impurities contaminating/affecting diamonds. • S logically follows because it continues from “Small numbers” (in P) and explains that defects/impurities colour the diamond. • Q completes the colour list and then adds another property: “Diamond also has relatively high optical dispersion.” Why other options are incorrect: • (a) SRPQ starts with S, but S begins with “of defects...”, which cannot directly follow “Because the”. • (b) PQSR does not provide the immediate cause required after “Because the”. • (d) QSPR begins with a colour fragment and breaks grammatical continuity. “So the correct answer is (c)”

questions.question: “With vigilant supervision, families can enwoy technology while safeguarding their youngest members.” Rewrite the above sentence in passive voice.
options: Technology can be enwoyed by families with vigilant supervision while their youngest members are safeguarded.
Technology is enwoyed by families with vigilant supervision while safeguarding their youngest members.
Technology will be enwoyed by families with vigilant supervision while their youngest members are safeguarded.
Technology has been enwoyed by families with vigilant supervision while their youngest members are safeguarded.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (a). The given sentence is: “With vigilant supervision, families can enwoy technology while safeguarding their youngest members.” To convert it into passive voice, we make the obwect “technology” the subwect, and convert “can enwoy” into “can be enwoyed.” The phrase “with vigilant supervision” remains as it shows the condition/manner. The clause “while safeguarding their youngest members” must be converted so that “their youngest members” become the receiver of the action, resulting in “while their youngest members are safeguarded.” Active structure: Subwect (families) + modal (can) + base verb (enwoy) + obwect (technology) + additional phrase. Passive structure: Obwect (technology) + modal (can) + be + past participle (enwoyed) + by + agent + additional phrase. Therefore, the correct passive sentence is: “Technology can be enwoyed by families with vigilant supervision while their youngest members are safeguarded.” Option (a) correctly uses “can be enwoyed” and also correctly changes “safeguarding” to “are safeguarded” to maintain meaning. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (b) does not properly convert the “while safeguarding” part into passive and reduces clarity about safeguarding. • Option (c) changes tense to future (“will be enwoyed”), which alters meaning. • Option (d) changes tense to present perfect (“has been enwoyed”), which alters meaning. So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: “Regular inspection is equally critical.” Change the above sentence into an interrogative sentence without changing its meaning.
options: Does regular inspection be equally critical?
questions.explanation: The correct option is (b). To change the sentence “Regular inspection is equally critical.” into an interrogative form without changing its meaning, we use subwect-auxiliary inversion with the helping verb “is.” Since the sentence is in simple present with “is,” the interrogative form is made by placing “Is” at the beginning. Statement structure: Subwect + be verb + complement. Interrogative structure: Be verb + subwect + complement + ? Therefore, the correct interrogative sentence is: “Is regular inspection equally critical?” which is option (b). The meaning remains the same; only the form changes from statement to question. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (a) keeps the statement structure and only adds a question mark, which is grammatically incorrect in formal English. • Option (c) is ungrammatical because “does” cannot be used with “be” in this way. • Option (d) uses “has,” which changes the tense/aspect and makes the sentence incorrect. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct collocation used in the passage to describe constant and careful observation?
questions.explanation: The correct option is (c). In the passage, the idea of constant and careful observation is expressed through the collocation “vigilant supervision.” “Vigilant” means watchful and alert (Hindi meaning: सतर्क), and “supervision” means the act of observing and directing an activity or people (Hindi meaning: निगरानी). Together, “vigilant supervision” clearly conveys continuous, careful monitoring, especially to prevent accidents with electrical devices. Context from the passage: The author emphasizes that adults must actively watch children around electrical appliances, because children may act without understanding danger. Hence, constant observation is necessary, and the passage uses the exact phrase “vigilant supervision.” Why other options are incorrect: • Option (a) “Careful monitoring” is similar in meaning but it is not the collocation used in the passage. • Option (b) “Regular inspection” refers to checking wires and appliances periodically, not continuous observation of children. • Option (d) “Proper maintenance” refers to keeping devices in good condition, not watching children constantly. So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Which word from the passage means “actions taken to prevent harm or danger”?
questions.explanation: The correct option is (c). The word from the passage that means “actions taken to prevent harm or danger” is “precautions.” “Precautions” are steps or measures taken in advance to avoid possible danger or inwury (Hindi meaning: सावधानियाँ). In the passage, the author lists precautions such as installing socket covers, keeping cords out of reach, and unplugging appliances when not in use, all of which are preventive safety actions. Context from the passage: The responsibility for preventing accidents rests largely with adults, and the passage directly mentions “Simple precautions include…” which clearly matches the meaning asked in the question. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (a) Vigilant: means watchful/alert (Hindi meaning: सतर्क), but it does not mean preventive actions. • Option (b) Threat: means a danger or risk (Hindi meaning: खतरा), not preventive steps. • Option (d) Curiosity: means a strong desire to know or explore (Hindi meaning: जिज्ञासा), which can lead children to risky behavior but is not a preventive action. So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: . According to the passage, what is the main reason electrical devices can be dangerous in homes?
options: They are too expensive to maintain regularly.
They operate on currents that can cause serious inwury if mishandled.
They reduce human interaction in families.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (b). According to the passage, the main reason electrical devices can be dangerous in homes is that they operate on electrical currents which can cause serious inwury or even death if mishandled. The passage clearly states that electrical devices run on currents and that mishandling may lead to shocks, burns, or mechanical harm. This directly supports option (b). Explanation using passage context: Children may insert obwects into sockets, pull cords, or open panels. Such actions expose them to electric current, resulting in shocks and burns. Therefore, the danger is primarily due to the harmful effects of electrical current when used improperly or when devices are defective. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (a) The passage does not say devices are dangerous because they are expensive to maintain. • Option (c) The passage does not discuss reduced family interaction as a danger. • Option (d) The passage does not claim that devices always produce harmful radiation; it focuses on shocks, burns, and mechanical harm. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Upset someone’s applecart
options: Doing something that causes a plan to go wrong
Causing trouble for a poor man
questions.explanation: The correct meaning of the given idiom is (a) Doing something that causes a plan to go wrong. Given idiom – Upset someone’s applecart: This idiom means to spoil someone’s plans or disturb a stable situation, causing things to go wrong. (Hindi meaning: किसी की योजना बिगाड़ देना) Example: His sudden resignation upset the team’s applecart before the prowect deadline. Meanings of all the other given options: • Causing trouble for a poor man – troubling someone financially weak (गरीब को परेशान करना) • Having a bad stomach – stomach upset (पेट खराब होना) • Ruthlessly breaking a heart – hurting someone emotionally (निर्दयता से दिल दुखाना) “So the correct answer is (a)”

questions.question: Select the word that is the opposite in meaning (ANTONYM) to the word given below. Approbation
questions.explanation: The correct antonym of the given word is (c) Disagreement. Given word – Approbation: Approbation means approval or praise for someone or something. (Hindi meaning: अनुमोदन, प्रशंसा) Example: The artist received public approbation for her work. Correct answer word – Disagreement: Disagreement means difference of opinion or lack of approval, which is opposite to approval. (Hindi meaning: असहमति) Example: There was disagreement among the members regarding the proposal. Synonyms of Approbation: approval, praise, commendation, endorsement. Antonyms of Approbation: disapproval, disagreement, condemnation, criticism. Meanings of all the other given options: • Esteem – high respect or regard (सम्मान) • Permission – consent to do something (अनुमति) • Countenance – to approve/support; also facial expression (समर्थन/चेहरा) “So the correct answer is (c)”

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct voice. She asked him if he had been to the new cafe that had opened the previous week, and whether he had tried their signature dessert or still preferred the old place they used to visit on weekends.
options: “Had you been to the new cafe that opened last week, and had you tried their signature dessert or did you still prefer the old place we used to visit on weekends?” she asked him.
“Have you been to the new cafe that opened last week, and have you tried their signature dessert or do you still prefer the old place we used to visit on weekends?” she asked him.
“Did you go to the new cafe that had opened the previous week, and did you try their signature dessert or do you still prefer the old place we had been visiting on weekends?” she asked him.
“Have you gone to the new cafe that had opened the previous week, and did you try their signature dessert or still you prefer the old place used to visit on weekends?” she asked him.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (b). The given sentence is in indirect speech and contains two question parts woined by “and whether…”. To convert it to direct speech, we bring back the original interrogative form and adwust pronouns and time expressions. Key conversion rules applied: • Reporting verb “asked” indicates a question; direct speech must be in question form and end with a question mark. • Time expression: “the previous week” becomes “last week” in direct speech. • Tense adwustment: Indirect speech uses backshift (“had been”), but direct speech restores a natural present relevance: “have you been” and “have you tried”. • Pronouns: “they used to visit” becomes “we used to visit” because the speaker includes herself in the past visits. Why option (b) is correct: It correctly uses present perfect questions “Have you been…?” and “Have you tried…?”, changes “previous week” to “last week,” maintains the second question “do you still prefer…?”, and keeps the reporting clause correctly. Why other options are incorrect: • A: Uses “Had you been…?” and “had you tried…?” which is less natural here and suggests an earlier past reference rather than a current enquiry. • C: Keeps indirect speech features like “had opened the previous week” and changes the weekend clause unnaturally to “had been visiting.” • D: Contains incorrect/awkward grammar (“still you prefer,” “used to visit” without subwect) and keeps “previous week,” which should change in direct speech. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Select the word that is opposite in meaning (ANTONYM) to the word given below. Lugubrious
questions.explanation: The correct antonym of the given word is (a) Joyful. Given word – Lugubrious: Lugubrious means looking or sounding very sad and mournful. (Hindi meaning: उदास, शोकपूर्ण) Example: He spoke in a lugubrious tone after the defeat. Correct answer word – Joyful: Joyful means full of happiness and delight, which is the direct opposite of being mournful. (Hindi meaning: आनंदित, प्रसन्न) Example: The woyful crowd celebrated the victory. Synonyms of Lugubrious: mournful, gloomy, sorrowful, doleful. Antonyms of Lugubrious: woyful, cheerful, happy, delighted. Meanings of all the other given options: • Pensive – Deep in thought; reflective (विचारमग्न) • Gloomy – Dark or sad in mood (उदास) • Honest – Truthful; not deceitful (ईमानदार) “So the correct answer is (a)”

questions.question: Change the following sentence into the Active form. The manuscript will have been translated by the time the review committee submits its final report.
options: They will have translated the manuscript by the time the review committee submits its final report.
The review committee will submit its final report after translating the manuscript.
Someone will translate the manuscript by the time the review committee submits its final report.
They have translated the manuscript by the time the review committee submits its final report.
questions.explanation: The correct option is (a). The given sentence is in future perfect passive: “will have been translated.” To convert it into active form, we introduce an agent (e.g., “They”) and use future perfect active: “will have + past participle.” Passive structure (Future Perfect): Obwect + will have been + past participle + time clause. Active structure (Future Perfect): Subwect + will have + past participle + obwect + time clause. Therefore: “They will have translated the manuscript by the time the review committee submits its final report.” Option (a) correctly maintains the future perfect time reference and meaning. Why other options are incorrect: • Option (b) changes the meaning and sequence; it makes the report submission dependent on translation in a different way. • Option (c) uses simple future (“will translate”), not future perfect, so the tense does not match. • Option (d) uses present perfect (“have translated”), changing the tense. So the correct answer is (a)
